Q32: We have live in Santa Barbara, CA for 40 years. Over that time we have seen many changes in the ocean; temperature increase, more beach pollution, water pollution, more marine mammal starvation and illness due to domoic acid poisoning, more animals needing care for entanglement s. I did marine mammal rescue and rehabilitation, so I’ve seen the changes with my own eyes. Certainly there have been more changes in the sea star population, shark populations, kelp health… on land we have much hotter summers, with seasonal high winds arriving earlier and leaving later. Droughts are more common. We now mow our open spaces all year long because of fire danger, and we have removed most plants that are not fire resistant. There has bees drop in all of the bird and animals species that we used to see and hear. The deer and coyotes are often in poor health. Coyotes often have mange. There are some bird species that I never see anymore. We have been evacuated twice from fires danger. To the good, we now only have electric cars, 30 solar panels, 3 battery walls, and have moved from propane to electric poser for all but winter heating and water heating. Propane tanks tend to explode when touched by fire.
